key_insight: This survey organizes LLM reasoning into a structured taxonomy and shows that stronger reasoning remains inconsistent across tasks, prompts, and model scales.
engineering_relevance: It helps computational scientists choose and evaluate reasoning methods more systematically when building robust, interpretable LLM-based problem-solving systems.
method: Reviews 300+ recent papers from arXiv, Semantic Scholar, Google Scholar, Papers with Code, and the ACL Anthology
Maps reasoning research across paradigms such as chain-of-thought, multi-hop, mathematical, multimodal, tool-augmented, and reinforcement learning-based reasoning
Synthesizes methods, benchmarks, and common failure modes into a unified framework
evidence_signal: The survey is grounded in a large literature review of more than 300 papers and compares reasoning methods using reported benchmarks and evaluation practices across domains.
watch_item: Reasoning performance is often brittle and prompt-sensitive, so apparent gains may not generalize reliably across tasks or domains.
paper_title: The Periodic Table of LLM Reasoning: A Structured Survey of Reasoning Paradigms, Methods, and Failure Modes
